@@ -0,0 +1,31 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '80'
+project: ai-panel
+title: WebSocket transport for the ACP agent server
+---
+## What
+The AI panel's ACP client runs entirely in the webview (TypeScript). A webview cannot spawn a subprocess or use stdio, so it cannot use ACP's standard stdio transport. The Rust ACP agent server must expose its JSON-RPC message stream over a transport a browser connects to natively: a **WebSocket**. Tauri is not involved in the ACP data path at all.
+
+- `sah agent acp` (`apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/agent/acp.rs`) currently calls `AcpServer::start_with_streams(stdin(), stdout())`. Add a WebSocket listen mode — e.g. `sah agent acp --listen 127.0.0.1:0`: bind a loopback TCP listener, accept a WebSocket upgrade, and run the ACP agent with the WebSocket carrying newline-delimited JSON-RPC (the same ndjson framing as stdio per ACP's transports spec — messages delimited by `\n`, no embedded newlines).
+- Use `tokio-tungstenite` (workspace dep). Adapt the WebSocket to the stream shape `AcpServer` needs, or add `start_with_websocket`.
+- Report the bound port (stdout line and/or exit-on-failure). Loopback-only bind.
+- stdio mode stays the default and is unchanged.
+
+This is the "server in Rust exposes the stream" half of the architecture. Reference: ACP transports — https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/transports
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `sah agent acp --listen 127.0.0.1:0` binds a loopback port, accepts a WebSocket connection, and serves ACP JSON-RPC over it.
+- [ ] ndjson framing over the WebSocket matches the stdio transport (newline-delimited, no embedded newlines).
+- [ ] The bound port is discoverable by the launcher (printed or otherwise surfaced).
+- [ ] stdio mode is unchanged and remains the default; `cargo build` clean.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Integration test: start `sah agent acp --listen 127.0.0.1:0`, connect a WebSocket client, send `initialize`, assert a valid ACP `initialize` response with negotiated protocol version.
+- [ ] Test that stdio mode (`start_with_streams`) still works unchanged.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-cli` (and the affected agent crate) is green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the WebSocket `initialize` round-trip test first.
\ No newline at end of file

+- `agent_ws::AgentWebSocketServer` is built but not yet started from the Tauri `setup_app` hook — wiring it into app startup and handing the bound port to the webview is the follow-up task (`01KRRN3SP5D1H63TQ8HM7SQZ1F`). Until then `ai/mod.rs` carries a module-wide `#![allow(dead_code)]`.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-05-16 16:40)
+
+Mode: task-mode. Scope: branch `kanban` vs `main` — `apps/kanban-app/src/ai/agent_ws.rs`, `apps/kanban-app/src/ai/mod.rs`, `apps/kanban-app/src/main.rs`, `apps/kanban-app/Cargo.toml`, `apps/kanban-app/tests/agent_ws.rs`. Verified: `cargo build -p kanban-app` clean with and without `--features ai-local-models`; `cargo test -p kanban-app` green (16 tests, the `agent_ws` integration test included); `cargo clippy -p kanban-app --tests` clean.
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/Cargo.toml:17-21` — The new `ai-local-models` Cargo feature contradicts an explicit documented constraint. `ARCHITECTURE.md` §1 Practices #1 states: "No feature flags. The Cargo workspace says explicitly: 'NEVER add features or feature flags.' The only exception is `test-support` for test utilities." `ai-local-models` is neither `test-support` nor exempt. Worse, the feature is inert: `swissarmyhammer-agent` depends on `llama-agent` unconditionally, so `llama-agent` compiles into `kanban-app` regardless of the flag (confirmed — a plain `cargo build -p kanban-app` compiles `llama-agent`). The feature gates no code, adds no dependency, and does not make the standard build lighter. The stated rationale ("so the standard build stays light") is therefore unmet. The "builds with and without the feature" acceptance criterion passes only because the feature is a no-op. Recommended fix: delete the `ai-local-models` feature entirely — `create_agent` already dispatches Claude vs llama at runtime via `ModelConfig::executor_type()`, so no compile-time gate is needed and the architecture rule is honored. If a genuine opt-out of the local-llama backend is wanted, that is a `swissarmyhammer-agent` restructuring task and should be tracked/justified against the no-feature-flags rule explicitly — not bolted on as an inert kanban-app-only flag.
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/src/ai/agent_ws.rs:84` — `bind_with` is `pub` and takes an arbitrary `ModelConfig`, but nothing in the crate or tests calls it (only `bind()` is used). It is kept alive solely by the module-wide `#![allow(dead_code)]` in `ai/mod.rs`. If it is genuinely needed for the follow-up wiring task (`01KRRN3SP5D1H63TQ8HM7SQZ1F`), fine; otherwise consider folding it into `bind()` until a caller exists, so the public surface reflects real use.
+- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/src/ai/agent_ws.rs:105` `run()` — the accept loop spawns one unbounded task per inbound connection with no cap. For a loopback-only dev/IPC server this is acceptable, but since any local process can open the ephemeral port, a connection cap (or at least a comment acknowledging the unbounded fan-out is intentional) would be worth adding when the follow-up task wires this into app startup.
+- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/src/ai/agent_ws.rs` — the loopback WebSocket has no origin/auth check, so any local process can connect and drive an in-process agent. This matches a desktop-app threat model and loopback binding limits exposure, but the follow-up wiring task should consider a per-launch token in the `ws://` URL handed to the webview, so only the app's own webview can connect.
\ No newline at end of file
